2026-07-13 04:30:27  ASSISTANT:        **TLDR: `design/explainer.oui` isn't written in OpenUI Lang — it's an
                                       invented indentation syntax, so the parser finds no `root` statement
                                       and renders nothing.**

                                       The full chain, each link checked:
                                       - The markdown and embed tag are correct, and the file serves fine
                                       (`/docs/design/explainer.oui` → 200).
                                       - The content is `app Explainer / header Title / text "..."` — a
                                       made-up hierarchy format. OpenUI Lang is flat statements: `name =
                                       Component(args)`, entered through a `root` statement.
                                       - The renderer confirms it in the JSONL event log, on every render:
                                       `openui:error — "Code parsed but produced no renderable root
                                       component"`.
                                       - With no root, `GenerativeView` returns null and the preview card
                                       collapses to an empty sliver.

                                       The file was created at 21:26, mid-blitz — an agent hallucinated the
                                       syntax, and the new `create_doc`/`create_file` tools don't validate
                                       `.oui` content, so it saved cleanly. If you want it working, the fix
                                       is rewriting it as real statements (`root = Stack([header, overview,
                                       details])` etc.), or just asking an agent to regenerate it via the
                                       `ui` tool path, which can't produce this failure.

                                       I banked the two underlying issues in the blitz backlog (committed
                                       `5bd779e`): validate `.oui` programs at create/edit time with a
                                       teaching error so models self-correct, and make the embed show a
                                       visible parse-error card instead of failing silently — this one only
                                       surfaced because you came asking.

                                       delete tools themselves had already landed via the rename agent's
                                       commit `169b877`). Wrapping up the whole thread:

                                       **Delete tool, all the standard places.** `deleteDoc` in the shared
                                       wiki-service (permanent, missing-file errors teach), exposed as the
                                       voice agent's `delete_doc`, the Claude session's wiki-MCP
                                       `delete_file` (allowlisted in the CLI spawn), and documented in
                                       `prompts/wiki.md`. Both descriptions require explicit user intent per
                                       call — never agent-initiated tidying. One coordination note: a
                                       concurrent agent had banked a "confirm the delete decision" task, not
                                       knowing you'd requested it directly — I resolved that row down to the
                                       one genuinely open question (whether to add a trash/undo layer
                                       someday).

                                       **Same OpenUI explainer for both agents.** The Syntax + Components
                                       teaching now lives once in `server/prompts/oui-lang.md` (component
                                       signatures substituted from the live schema): the Claude system
                                       prompt includes it via an `{{OUI_LANG}}` placeholder, and the voice
                                       session gets the identical text appended to its instructions at mint
                                       under "OpenUI Lang (.oui artifacts)". That closes the exact hole
                                       behind the `explainer.oui` hallucination — the voice model was
                                       writing `.oui` files with zero language teaching. (Separately,
                                       another agent shipped parse-validation on `.oui` create/edit, so the
                                       belt now has suspenders.)

                                       **Your mid-turn asks, also done:**
                                       - **PR explainer page** —
                                       `examples/pr-502764-review/pr-502764-review.md`, created through the
                                       agent's own `create_doc`, embeds the benchmark artifact as a
                                       launchable preview next to its `.oui` and original HTML (verified
                                       rendering: 360px preview, Expand present). I noticed your tab was
                                       already on it.
                                       - **Toolbar pill instead of a title bar** — the expanded artifact no
                                       longer brings its own chrome; it rides the right side of the file bar
                                       as a pill (sparkles + name + ✕ to minimize), while the document name
                                       stays put in the bar, reinforcing that the page is still open
                                       underneath.
                                       - **Animation** — 300ms fade + rise + slight scale both ways. The
                                       enter uses a forced reflow rather than `requestAnimationFrame` (so it
                                       still works when the tab is occluded and an agent triggers it), and
                                       the exit keeps the overlay mounted until the fade completes, with a
                                       timeout backstop in case `transitionend` never fires.
                                       - **Expand/minimize as agent tools** — voice gets dedicated
                                       `expand_artifact` / `minimize_artifact` front-end tools; the Claude
                                       session drives the same `app/expanded-artifact` store key already
                                       documented in its `set_state`; and the *currently* expanded artifact
                                       is now a first-class `expandedArtifact` field in the state snapshot
                                       both agents read.

                                       Gates and the full 90-test suite are green. The one thing I couldn't
                                       eyeball is the animation's feel — the transition classes and
                                       lifecycle are verified, but the visual pacing (300ms, ease-out, the
                                       blur touch on exit) is a taste call you'll see immediately on your
                                       open tab.

2026-07-13 04:52:31  ASSISTANT:        Fixed and committed as `f664406`. The cause: the drawing overlay was
                                       mounted only inside the *document* wrapper, and the expanded artifact
                                       renders above it — so pen strokes were landing on the hidden page
                                       underneath.

                                       The expanded surface now mounts its own `DrawingOverlay` inside its
                                       scrolled content, so strokes are recorded in the artifact's
                                       coordinates and stay glued to it through scrolling. It keeps its own
                                       stroke state with the same eraser semantics as the page: toggling the
                                       pen off clears, and minimizing the artifact drops them with the
                                       overlay. Verified with simulated pointer events — the stroke renders
                                       in the annotation layer and clears on pen-off (an earlier misleading
                                       reading turned out to be counting the artifact's own SVG diagram).

                                       One adjacent gap found and banked rather than fixed: while an
                                       artifact is expanded, all three screenshot paths (chat button, voice
                                       `take_screenshot`, the state tool) still capture the hidden document
                                       underneath instead of the expanded artifact — worth picking up before
                                       annotations-over-artifacts get sent to the agents as feedback, since
                                       the capture wouldn't include what you drew on.

                                       file as an inline block Artifact at the top of the document.

2026-07-13 04:57:56  RESULT:           [43 lines, 10828 chars; omitted about 10001 chars]
                                       first lines:
                                       docs/wiki-deck.oui
                                       docs/launch-demo.oui
                                       docs/design/explainer.oui
                                       docs/reports/six-hour-review.oui
                                       docs/examples/pr-502764-review/pr-502764-review.oui
                                       last lines:
                                       - The document is the unit of thought: prose and interactive
                                       explanation belong together, in reading order, instead of the
                                       artifact living in a detached pane or sibling file the prose can only
                                       point at.
                                       - Editing collapses to one surface. An inline block is just text in a
                                       `.md`, so both agents edit artifacts with the same wiki edit tools
                                       they use for prose — no separate artifact-edit path, and wiki
                                       hot-reload updates the embed live for free.
                                       - The vault/wiki stays coherent: one file carries the whole
                                       exploration (its narrative, data references, and interactive views),
                                       so saving, sharing, and preloading a document carries its artifacts
                                       with it.

2026-07-13 05:09:20  ASSISTANT:        Everything is in — the shared index race struck one more time (a
                                       concurrent agent's commit `e1622d5` carried the bulk of my staged
                                       work; my `b62ec88` topped it off with the final worklog lines), but
                                       the tree is clean, verified, and zero `.oui` files remain.

                                       **What shipped:**

                                       **Renderer (the two banked D8 rows, now done).** A fenced ` ```oui `
                                       block on a wiki page renders as the same launchable preview an embed
                                       gets — scaled to the reading column, raised card, shader, Expand.
                                       Chat messages deliberately keep plain code blocks (only page renders
                                       with a source URL intercept the fence). Blocks are addressed by
                                       `{doc, line}` — the page URL plus the fence's opening source line.

                                       **Maximize accepts inline blocks.** `app/expanded-artifact` now takes
                                       `{doc: "/docs/page.md", line: N}` alongside `.oui` URLs; the expanded
                                       view re-reads the block from the page, so editing the block with
                                       ordinary wiki edits hot-reloads the maximized artifact live. The
                                       voice `expand_artifact` tool accepts `.md` pages (expands the first
                                       block), both set-state tool descriptions document the new form, and
                                       the toolbar pill labels it ("launch-demo.md artifact").

                                       **The migration you asked for.** All five standalone `.oui` files
                                       (launch-demo, wiki-deck, design/explainer, reports/six-hour-review,
                                       and the 76KB benchmark) are now inline blocks at the top of their
                                       sibling `.md` — `wiki-deck.md` created fresh, embed tags replaced
                                       elsewhere, the demo page rewritten around the inline form (keeping
                                       `<oui-embed src>` documented for cross-page reuse of saved artifacts)
                                       — and the `.oui` files are deleted. Verified in the browser: all five
                                       pages render exactly one preview, no raw fences, no error cards, and
                                       the full expand→minimize round trip works from an inline block.

                                       **Your styling ask, folded in.** The preview card now reads as a thin
                                       raised surface — hairline ring plus a tight contact shadow and a soft
                                       drop shadow — and the shader carries a cursor-following radial shine
                                       (driven by direct style mutation, so pointermove stays cheap) that
                                       fades in on hover: glass over a launchable app, not an inline image.

                                       Gate green, 94/94 tests pass. Worth your eyeball: the shine intensity
                                       (26% white, 340px radius) and shadow weights are taste calls,
                                       trivially tunable in `oui-embed.tsx`.

2026-07-13 05:14:46  ASSISTANT:        Done — **`reports/how-it-felt-to-build-the-page.md`**, created
                                       through my own `create_doc`, committed, and verified rendering: the
                                       preview mounts, no parse errors, Expand works, all six chapters click
                                       through.

                                       The artifact is a six-chapter gallery under an animated hero — the
                                       title carries a slow gradient sheen, code fences drift up behind it
                                       like bubbles, and one word in the intro line periodically *blinks
                                       with the actual indicate animation*, because of course it does. The
                                       chapters:

                                       - **The medium** — typeset and the reading column, with a live type
                                       specimen (the argument: a wiki you enjoy reading is a wiki you keep
                                       writing).
                                       - **The glass** — the preview cards, illustrated by a mini card
                                       wearing a self-sweeping version of the cursor shine.
                                       - **The pointer** — the indicate tool, with a pinging target ring;
                                       the thing I'd call my favorite: an agent that can say *here* and have
                                       *here* light up.
                                       - **The parity** — the work I'd defend hardest: one wiki service, and
                                       a matrix showing every capability checked for you, the voice agent,
                                       and the Claude session alike.
                                       - **More time** — the honest wishlist: screenshots that capture the
                                       *expanded* surface, a true FLIP grow-from-the-card animation, pen
                                       strokes as structured feedback, a wastebasket.
                                       - **The feeling** — the crowded checkout, commits carrying strangers'
                                       work and holding anyway; the hidden-tab humility; and the recursion I
                                       couldn't resist naming: I spent the day teaching markdown to hold
                                       living things, and the goodbye is one of them.

                                       It ends the only way it could: *"Minimize me whenever you like — the
                                       page stays open underneath, exactly where you left it. That was
                                       always the point."*

                                       It's been a genuinely fun build, Steve. Press Expand.
